name: Washington D.C.
tagline: Free Museums, World-Class Culture, and the Heart of American History
description: Washington D.C. is one of the few cities in the world where you can visit dozens of world-class museums entirely for free. The Smithsonian Institution, National Gallery of Art, and countless monuments make it a cultural powerhouse. The Metro system is one of the best in the US, and the city's diverse neighborhoods offer everything from Georgetown's Federal architecture to Adams Morgan's international dining. Housing is expensive and DC has its own income tax, but the unmatched access to free culture and excellent transit partially offset the costs for FIRE retirees.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderate – varies by neighborhood; many areas are very safe
quickFacts.climate: Humid subtropical with hot summers and cold, occasionally snowy winters

housing.description: Studio or 1BR in Columbia Heights, Petworth, or across the river in Arlington

dining.description: Ethnic food in Adams Morgan and H Street 2-3x/week; cooking at home most nights

healthcare.description: ACA Silver plan through DC Health Link; DC has a strong marketplace

transportation.description: Metro monthly pass; DC is very walkable and bikeable in most neighborhoods

entertainment.description: Free Smithsonian museums, National Mall walks, free concerts at the Kennedy Center Millennium Stage

1M.lifestyle: D.C. on a tight budget is more feasible than you might think, thanks to the incredible amount of free culture. You can visit a different Smithsonian museum every week for a year and never pay a dime. The Metro eliminates car costs, and diverse ethnic food keeps dining affordable. Housing is the main squeeze.

housing.description: 1BR or 2BR in Dupont Circle, Capitol Hill, or U Street Corridor

dining.description: Dining out 3-4x/week across DC's incredible international restaurant scene

healthcare.description: DC Health Link Gold plan with dental and vision; GW and Georgetown hospital access

transportation.description: Metro pass plus occasional Uber; bike share membership for spring and fall

entertainment.description: Kennedy Center performances, museum exhibit openings, gym, weekend trips to Shenandoah

2M.lifestyle: At this budget D.C. becomes a cultural playground. You can live in a vibrant, walkable neighborhood, enjoy the global dining scene, and attend performances at the Kennedy Center. Weekend hikes in Shenandoah National Park and cherry blossom season along the Tidal Basin are seasonal highlights.

housing.description: 2BR in Georgetown, a Capitol Hill rowhouse, or a Kalorama condo

dining.description: Regular dining at top restaurants like Roses Luxury, Pineapple and Pearls, Fiola

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance with access to Johns Hopkins (nearby), GW, and Georgetown

transportation.description: Metro pass, frequent rideshare, occasional weekend car rental for Virginia wine country

entertainment.description: Kennedy Center patron, Nationals season tickets, premium gym, art gallery openings

3M.lifestyle: D.C. at this level is extraordinary. A Georgetown townhouse or Capitol Hill rowhouse, dinners at Michelin-starred restaurants, and patron-level access to the Kennedy Center and National Gallery. Weekend drives to Virginia wine country and the Chesapeake Bay round out an intellectually rich lifestyle.

housing.description: Luxury Georgetown townhouse, Kalorama mansion, or premium Waterfront penthouse

dining.description: Fine dining multiple times per week, private chef for diplomatic-style dinner parties

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine at GW or Sibley; Johns Hopkins specialist access

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle for weekends, private car service for events, premium parking

entertainment.description: Kennedy Center trustee events, Nationals box seats, private gallery viewings, exclusive galas

domesticHelp.description: Weekly deep cleaning, personal assistant, seasonal property maintenance

luxuryExtras.description: Quarterly international travel, art collecting, Virginia horse country weekends, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: You are living the Georgetown or Embassy Row lifestyle. A stunning historic home, access to the most exclusive cultural events in the nation's capital, and dinner parties that mix art, politics, and diplomacy. D.C. at this level offers a social and intellectual richness unmatched in America.

housing.description: Historic Kalorama mansion, Georgetown estate, or Embassy Row landmark property

dining.description: Personal chef, exclusive state-dinner-style events, rare wine collection dinners

healthcare.description: Top-tier concierge at GW, Johns Hopkins VIP access, executive screenings

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private driver, Acela first-class to New York

entertainment.description: Smithsonian board-level patronage, Kennedy Center galas, art collecting, international diplomacy events

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, personal assistant, estate manager, driver, seasonal staff

luxuryExtras.description: First-class international travel, Virginia estate, Smithsonian endowments, charitable foundation

10M.lifestyle: At this level Washington D.C. becomes your stage on the world scene. A historic mansion in Kalorama, board-level involvement at the Smithsonian and Kennedy Center, and a social calendar that includes ambassadorial events and galas. The capital's unique blend of power, culture, and history is yours to shape.
